title: Getting started with infosigasp
text: INFOSIGA-SP São Paulo State Traffic Accident Information Management System, maintained DETRAN-SP. publishes, open data, detailed records every traffic crash state São Paulo 2015 onward. infosigasp package wraps download import records. takes care things make raw files awkward read directly: files encoded Latin-1 (ISO-8859-1), UTF-8; fields separated semicolons (;); decimal numbers (coordinates) use comma decimal mark; dates formatted DD/MM/YYYY; dataset split across two files (2015–2021 2022 onward).
code: library(infosigasp)

title: Getting started with infosigasp
text: INFOSIGA-SP organises data three linked tables: sinistros — crash events. One row per recorded event, date, time, location (including latitude/longitude), road attributes breakdown many vehicles victims involved, type severity. pessoas — people (victims). One row per person involved, demographic attributes, injury severity , fatalities, date place death. veiculos — vehicles. One row per vehicle involved, make/model, manufacturing model years, colour type. three share id_sinistro key, can joined together; pessoas veiculos additionally share id_veiculo.
code: infosiga_datasets() #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   dataset   description                                              grain keys  #>   <chr>     <chr>                                                    <chr> <chr> #> 1 sinistros Traffic crash events recorded in the state of Sao Paulo. one … id_s… #> 2 pessoas   People (victims) involved in traffic crashes.            one … id_p… #> 3 veiculos  Vehicles involved in traffic crashes.                    one … id_v…

title: Getting started with infosigasp
text: main entry point read_infosiga(). first call downloads source archive (120 MB) per-user cache; subsequent calls read cache, pay download cost . can restrict import specific years year argument, filters year crash (ano_sinistro):
code: sinistros <- read_infosiga("sinistros") sinistros recent <- read_infosiga("sinistros", year = 2022:2025)

what: A peek at the structure without downloading
title: Getting started with infosigasp
text: package ships small sample dataset can inspect columns without network access:
code: sample_path <- system.file("extdata", "sinistros_sample.csv", package = "infosigasp") sample <- readr::read_delim(   sample_path,   delim = ";",   show_col_types = FALSE ) dim(sample) #> [1] 100  48 names(sample) #>  [1] "id_sinistro"                     "tipo_registro"                   #>  [3] "data_sinistro"                   "ano_sinistro"                    #>  [5] "mes_sinistro"                    "dia_sinistro"                    #>  [7] "hora_sinistro"                   "ano_mes_sinistro"                #>  [9] "dia_da_semana"                   "turno"                           #> [11] "logradouro"                      "numero_logradouro"               #> [13] "tipo_via"                        "tipo_local"                      #> [15] "latitude"                        "longitude"                       #> [17] "cod_ibge"                        "municipio"                       #> [19] "regiao_administrativa"           "administracao"                   #> [21] "conservacao"                     "circunscricao"                   #> [23] "tp_sinistro_primario"            "qtd_pedestre"                    #> [25] "qtd_bicicleta"                   "qtd_motocicleta"                 #> [27] "qtd_automovel"                   "qtd_onibus"                      #> [29] "qtd_caminhao"                    "qtd_veic_outros"                 #> [31] "qtd_veic_nao_disponivel"         "qtd_gravidade_fatal"             #> [33] "qtd_gravidade_grave"             "qtd_gravidade_leve"              #> [35] "qtd_gravidade_ileso"             "qtd_gravidade_nao_disponivel"    #> [37] "tp_sinistro_atropelamento"       "tp_sinistro_colisao_frontal"     #> [39] "tp_sinistro_colisao_traseira"    "tp_sinistro_colisao_lateral"     #> [41] "tp_sinistro_colisao_transversal" "tp_sinistro_colisao_outros"      #> [43] "tp_sinistro_choque"              "tp_sinistro_capotamento"         #> [45] "tp_sinistro_engavetamento"       "tp_sinistro_tombamento"          #> [47] "tp_sinistro_outros"              "tp_sinistro_nao_disponivel"

title: Getting started with infosigasp
text: imported, data ordinary tibbles, tidyverse (base R) workflow applies. example, counting traffic fatalities per year victims dataset: fatalities broken type victim (driver, passenger, pedestrian): sinistros carries latitude longitude numeric columns, crash locations can mapped directly aggregated municipality (municipio / cod_ibge).
code: library(dplyr)  deaths_by_year <- read_infosiga("pessoas") |>   filter(gravidade_lesao == "FATAL") |>   count(ano_obito, name = "deaths") |>   arrange(ano_obito)  deaths_by_year read_infosiga("pessoas") |>   filter(gravidade_lesao == "FATAL") |>   count(tipo_de_vitima, sort = TRUE)

title: Getting started with infosigasp
text: download lives operating-system specific cache directory: archive refreshed monthly DETRAN-SP. pull latest version, force re-download: reclaim disk space, clear cache: can point cache somewhere else session (permanently via .Rprofile) infosigasp.cache_dir option:
code: infosiga_cache_dir() #> [1] "/home/runner/.cache/R/infosigasp" infosiga_cache_list() #> character(0) infosiga_download(overwrite = TRUE) infosiga_cache_clear() options(infosigasp.cache_dir = "~/data/infosiga")

title: Getting started with infosigasp
text: INFOSIGA-SP distributes field--field data dictionary (PDF, Portuguese). infosiga_dictionary() downloads returns paths extracted files:
code: pdfs <- infosiga_dictionary() basename(pdfs)

title: Getting started with infosigasp
text: Data published DETRAN-SP Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 licence. publish results based data, please cite INFOSIGA-SP / DETRAN-SP source: https://infosiga.detran.sp.gov.br/.

what: Download and import traffic crash data from INFOSIGA-SP
title: Download and import traffic crash data from INFOSIGA-SP
text: infosigasp provides clean, programmatic interface open data published INFOSIGA-SP, São Paulo State Traffic Accident Information Management System maintained DETRAN-SP (São Paulo State Department Motor Vehicles). package downloads official data archive, handles quirks (Latin-1 encoding, semicolon separators, comma decimal marks, DD/MM/YYYY dates) returns tidy tibbles ready analysis. covers every traffic crash recorded state São Paulo 2015 onward.

title: Download and import traffic crash data from INFOSIGA-SP
text: can install development version GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("viniciusoike/infosigasp")

title: Download and import traffic crash data from INFOSIGA-SP
text: INFOSIGA-SP publishes three linked datasets: datasets can joined id_sinistro (id_veiculo, present).
code: library(infosigasp) infosiga_datasets() #> # A tibble: 3 x 4 #>   dataset   description                                              grain keys  #>   <chr>     <chr>                                                    <chr> <chr> #> 1 sinistros Traffic crash events recorded in the state of Sao Paulo. one ~ id_s~ #> 2 pessoas   People (victims) involved in traffic crashes.            one ~ id_p~ #> 3 veiculos  Vehicles involved in traffic crashes.                    one ~ id_v~

title: Download and import traffic crash data from INFOSIGA-SP
text: first call downloads source archive (~120 MB) local cache; later calls read straight disk.
code: library(infosigasp)  # Crash events (one row per event) sinistros <- read_infosiga("sinistros")  # Victims, restricted to recent years vitimas <- read_infosiga("pessoas", year = 2022:2025)  # Vehicles involved veiculos <- read_infosiga("veiculos")

title: Download and import traffic crash data from INFOSIGA-SP
code: infosiga_cache_dir()      # where files are stored infosiga_cache_list()     # what is currently cached infosiga_download(overwrite = TRUE)  # force a refresh after a monthly update infosiga_cache_clear()    # delete cached files

title: Download and import traffic crash data from INFOSIGA-SP
text: official field--field documentation (PDF, Portuguese) can fetched :
code: infosiga_dictionary()

title: Download and import traffic crash data from INFOSIGA-SP
text: small fatality summary year, using victims dataset:
code: library(dplyr)  read_infosiga("pessoas") |>   filter(gravidade_lesao == "FATAL") |>   count(ano_obito, name = "deaths") |>   arrange(ano_obito)

title: Download and import traffic crash data from INFOSIGA-SP
text: Data published DETRAN-SP Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 licence https://infosiga.detran.sp.gov.br/. using data, please cite INFOSIGA-SP / DETRAN-SP source. package released MIT licence affiliated endorsed DETRAN-SP Government State São Paulo.

what: Manage the infosigasp on-disk cache — infosiga_cache
title: Manage the infosigasp on-disk cache — infosiga_cache
text: INFOSIGA-SP ships data single archive roughly 120 MB (uncompressed, 700 MB). avoid repeated downloads, infosigasp stores archive per-user cache directory reuses across sessions. functions inspect manage cache.

title: Manage the infosigasp on-disk cache — infosiga_cache
code: infosiga_cache_dir()  infosiga_cache_list()  infosiga_cache_clear(confirm = interactive())

title: Manage the infosigasp on-disk cache — infosiga_cache
text: confirm Logical. TRUE (default interactive sessions), ask confirmation deleting cached files. Set FALSE delete without prompting (e.g. scripts).

title: Manage the infosigasp on-disk cache — infosiga_cache
text: infosiga_cache_dir() returns cache directory path (string), creating necessary. infosiga_cache_list() returns character vector cached file paths (possibly empty). infosiga_cache_clear() invisibly returns paths removed.

title: Manage the infosigasp on-disk cache — infosiga_cache
text: cache location defaults operating-system specific user cache directory returned tools::R_user_dir() ("infosigasp", "cache"). can override current session infosigasp.cache_dir option, e.g. options(infosigasp.cache_dir = "~/-cache"), permanently .Rprofile.

title: Manage the infosigasp on-disk cache — infosiga_cache
code: # Where does infosigasp cache its files? infosiga_cache_dir() #> [1] "/home/runner/.cache/R/infosigasp"  # What is currently cached? infosiga_cache_list() #> character(0)

what: List the available INFOSIGA-SP datasets — infosiga_datasets
title: List the available INFOSIGA-SP datasets — infosiga_datasets
text: Returns small tibble describing datasets read_infosiga() can import, including grain (one row represents) key columns.

title: List the available INFOSIGA-SP datasets — infosiga_datasets
code: infosiga_datasets()

title: List the available INFOSIGA-SP datasets — infosiga_datasets
text: tibble columns dataset, description, grain keys.

title: List the available INFOSIGA-SP datasets — infosiga_datasets
code: infosiga_datasets() #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   dataset   description                                              grain keys  #>   <chr>     <chr>                                                    <chr> <chr> #> 1 sinistros Traffic crash events recorded in the state of Sao Paulo. one … id_s… #> 2 pessoas   People (victims) involved in traffic crashes.            one … id_p… #> 3 veiculos  Vehicles involved in traffic crashes.                    one … id_v…

what: Download the INFOSIGA-SP data dictionary — infosiga_dictionary
title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP data dictionary — infosiga_dictionary
text: Downloads official INFOSIGA-SP data dictionary, set PDF documents (one per dataset) describing every column accepted values. archive saved cache extracted PDF paths returned.

title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP data dictionary — infosiga_dictionary
code: infosiga_dictionary(   dest = file.path(infosiga_cache_dir(), "dictionary"),   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP data dictionary — infosiga_dictionary
text: dest Directory extract PDF files. Defaults dictionary sub-folder infosiga_cache_dir(). overwrite Logical. Re-download even dictionary archive already cached. Defaults FALSE. quiet Logical. Suppress progress messages. Defaults FALSE.

title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP data dictionary — infosiga_dictionary
text: character vector paths extracted PDF files, invisibly.

title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP data dictionary — infosiga_dictionary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pdfs <- infosiga_dictionary() # Open the dictionary for the crash-events dataset browseURL(grep("sinistros", pdfs, value = TRUE)) } # }

what: Download the INFOSIGA-SP source archive — infosiga_download
title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP source archive — infosiga_download
text: Downloads consolidated INFOSIGA-SP data archive (dados_infosiga.zip) DETRAN-SP local cache. users need call directly: read_infosiga() downloads archive demand. Use function want pre-fetch data (example, going offline) force refresh.

title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP source archive — infosiga_download
code: infosiga_download(overwrite = FALSE, quiet = FALSE, timeout = 3600)

title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP source archive — infosiga_download
text: overwrite Logical. FALSE (default) archive already cached, existing file kept returned. Set TRUE download replace . quiet Logical. FALSE (default), report progress informative messages. timeout Download timeout seconds. archive large (around 120 MB), default temporarily raises options()$timeout 3600. Pass larger value slow connections.

title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP source archive — infosiga_download
text: path cached archive, invisibly.

title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP source archive — infosiga_download
text: archive updated monthly DETRAN-SP accumulates records 2015 onward. download URL can overridden infosigasp.zip_url option, mainly useful testing.

title: Download the INFOSIGA-SP source archive — infosiga_download
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Pre-fetch the archive into the cache infosiga_download()  # Force a refresh after a monthly update infosiga_download(overwrite = TRUE) } # }

what: infosigasp: Download and Import Traffic Crash Data from INFOSIGA-SP — infosigasp-package
title: infosigasp: Download and Import Traffic Crash Data from INFOSIGA-SP — infosigasp-package
text: infosigasp package provides programmatic interface open data published Sao Paulo State Traffic Accident Information Management System (INFOSIGA-SP), maintained Sao Paulo State Department Motor Vehicles (DETRAN-SP).

title: infosigasp: Download and Import Traffic Crash Data from INFOSIGA-SP — infosigasp-package
text: INFOSIGA-SP distributes three related datasets covering every traffic crash recorded state Sao Paulo, Brazil, 2015 onward: sinistros Crash events: one row per recorded event, date, time, location breakdown vehicles victims severity. pessoas Victims: one row per person involved, demographic attributes, injury severity , fatalities, date death. veiculos Vehicles: one row per vehicle involved, make, model, year type. three datasets can linked id_sinistro key (id_veiculo, applicable).

title: infosigasp: Download and Import Traffic Crash Data from INFOSIGA-SP — infosigasp-package
text: read_infosiga() Download (needed) import dataset tibble. infosiga_download() Download raw source archive local cache. infosiga_cache_dir(), infosiga_cache_list(), infosiga_cache_clear() Manage -disk cache.

title: infosigasp: Download and Import Traffic Crash Data from INFOSIGA-SP — infosigasp-package
text: Data published DETRAN-SP Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 licence https://infosiga.detran.sp.gov.br/. package affiliated endorsed DETRAN-SP Government State Sao Paulo.

title: infosigasp: Download and Import Traffic Crash Data from INFOSIGA-SP — infosigasp-package
text: Maintainer: Vinicius Oike viniciusoike@gmail.com [copyright holder]

title: Import an INFOSIGA-SP dataset — read_infosiga
text: Downloads (necessary) imports one three INFOSIGA-SP datasets tidy tibble. source archive cached locally, first call triggers download subsequent calls read disk.

title: Import an INFOSIGA-SP dataset — read_infosiga
code: read_infosiga(   dataset = c("sinistros", "pessoas", "veiculos"),   year = NULL,   download_if_missing = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   ... )

title: Import an INFOSIGA-SP dataset — read_infosiga
text: dataset dataset import. One : "sinistros" Crash events (one row per event). "pessoas" Victims / people involved (one row per person). "veiculos" Vehicles involved (one row per vehicle). year Optional integer vector used filter rows year crash (ano_sinistro). NULL (default), available years returned. example, year = 2020:2023. download_if_missing Logical. TRUE (default), download archive already cached. FALSE archive missing, informative error raised. quiet Logical. FALSE (default), report progress. ... Additional arguments passed infosiga_download() (example overwrite = TRUE force refresh).

title: Import an INFOSIGA-SP dataset — read_infosiga
text: tibble one row per record. columns keep original INFOSIGA-SP names (Portuguese); see package data dictionary via infosiga_dictionary(). three datasets can joined id_sinistro (id_veiculo, present).

title: Import an INFOSIGA-SP dataset — read_infosiga
text: Source files encoded Latin-1 (ISO-8859-1), use ; field separator, , decimal mark DD/MM/YYYY dates. read_infosiga() handles returns UTF-8 text, Date columns numeric coordinates. dataset distributed across two period files inside archive (2015-2021 2022 onward); read row-bound transparently. imported data follow source closely possible otherwise cleaned. small fraction rows source contain data-quality issues (example, unescaped ; inside street name, mis-encoded latitude/longitude values); coordinates particular sanity-checked mapping. value parsed declared column type set NA recorded readr::problems(). Empty fields read NA. Note many categorical columns instead use literal value "NAO DISPONIVEL" ("available") flag missing information; preserved -imported data matches source. crash-type flag columns (tp_sinistro_*) hold "S" flag applies NA otherwise.

title: Import an INFOSIGA-SP dataset — read_infosiga
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Import all crash events (downloads the archive on first use) sinistros <- read_infosiga("sinistros")  # Only victims from 2022 and 2023 vitimas <- read_infosiga("pessoas", year = 2022:2023) } # }  # A bundled sample (no download required) illustrates the structure: sample_path <- system.file(   "extdata", "sinistros_sample.csv",   package = "infosigasp" ) if (nzchar(sample_path)) head(readr::read_delim(sample_path, ";")) #> Rows: 100 Columns: 48 #> ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Delimiter: ";" #> chr  (25): tipo_registro, data_sinistro, mes_sinistro, dia_sinistro, ano_mes... #> dbl  (13): id_sinistro, ano_sinistro, numero_logradouro, cod_ibge, qtd_pedes... #> num   (2): latitude, longitude #> lgl   (7): qtd_bicicleta, qtd_caminhao, qtd_veic_outros, qtd_gravidade_ileso... #> time  (1): hora_sinistro #>  #> ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. #> ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. #> # A tibble: 6 × 48 #>   id_sinistro tipo_registro data_sinistro ano_sinistro mes_sinistro dia_sinistro #>         <dbl> <chr>         <chr>                <dbl> <chr>        <chr>        #> 1     1265457 NOTIFICACAO   01/01/2022            2022 01           01           #> 2     1301846 NOTIFICACAO   01/01/2022            2022 01           01           #> 3     1352130 NOTIFICACAO   01/01/2022            2022 01           01           #> 4     2279865 NOTIFICACAO   01/01/2022            2022 01           01           #> 5     1444289 NOTIFICACAO   01/01/2022            2022 01           01           #> 6     1322053 SINISTRO NAO… 01/01/2022            2022 01           01           #> # ℹ 42 more variables: hora_sinistro <time>, ano_mes_sinistro <chr>, #> #   dia_da_semana <chr>, turno <chr>, logradouro <chr>, #> #   numero_logradouro <dbl>, tipo_via <chr>, tipo_local <chr>, latitude <dbl>, #> #   longitude <dbl>, cod_ibge <dbl>, municipio <chr>, #> #   regiao_administrativa <chr>, administracao <chr>, conservacao <chr>, #> #   circunscricao <chr>, tp_sinistro_primario <chr>, qtd_pedestre <dbl>, #> #   qtd_bicicleta <lgl>, qtd_motocicleta <dbl>, qtd_automovel <dbl>, …

title: infosigasp 0.1.0
text: Initial release. read_infosiga() downloads (caching) imports three INFOSIGA-SP datasets (sinistros, pessoas, veiculos) tidy tibbles, handling source encoding, decimal marks date formats. infosiga_download() pre-fetches source archive local cache. infosiga_cache_dir(), infosiga_cache_list() infosiga_cache_clear() manage -disk cache. infosiga_datasets() lists available datasets keys. infosiga_dictionary() downloads official data dictionary.
